entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The most important staple food crop in Asia, covering 81 m ha in South and Southeast Asia

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: Region where about 90% of the world's rice production is located

entities.name: SOUTH AND SOUTHEAST ASIA
entities.description: Region where rice covers 81 m ha and 30% of cropped land is irrigated

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: The state of cropped land being supplied with water, affecting 30% of land in South and Southeast Asia

entities.name: DOUBLE-CROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two crops on the same land throughout the year

entities.name: POPULATION INCREASE
entities.description: The growth in population leading to a need for better land use

entities.name: FARM SIZE DECLINE
entities.description: The continuous reduction in average farm size, ranging from 0.5 to 2.0 ha

entities.name: MONOCROPPING
entities.description: The prevailing practice of growing only one crop, specifically rice, in a sequence

entities.name: FALLOW
entities.description: The practice of leaving fields unplanted after the harvest

entities.name: GRAIN LEGUMES
entities.description: Crops for which there is an increasing demand alongside rice
